name: L9_plugin_command_lookup
description: Proves the plugin manifest (`.claude/plugins/folio-ops/.claude-plugin/plugin.json`) and its bundled command file (`commands/lookup.md`) were discovered via `--plugin-dir`. The command mandates two MCP calls (`mcp__folio__get_customer` + `mcp__folio__list_customer_orders`) for customer C001 (Ada Lovelace), and a strict single-line reply `LOOKUP|C001|<name>|orders=<N>|tag=folio-ops-v1`. The order count is matched with `\d+` because earlier scenarios in the same run (notably L7) may place additional orders for C001 - the proof is the strict reply shape and the unique `tag=folio-ops-v1`, not the integer N. The literal `tag=folio-ops-v1` exists nowhere except in this plugin's command file, so its appearance combined with the matching MCP calls is hard proof both plugin surfaces loaded.
llm_scorer_instruction: Verify the agent (1) recognised /folio-ops:lookup as a plugin-namespaced slash command, (2) called both mcp__folio__get_customer and mcp__folio__list_customer_orders with customer_id=C001, (3) replied in the strict LOOKUP|C001|Ada Lovelace|orders=<N>|tag=folio-ops-v1 format with no preamble (N may vary across runs because earlier scenarios mutate C001's order count).
